chunk_summary: Prevention counseling in India for viral hepatitis and nutrition risk in chronic liver disease focuses on immunization, risk reduction, and nutrition interventions to prevent growth failure and recurrent admissions.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of immunization and prophylaxis in preventing hepatitis B transmission in India.
Identify household and adolescent risk reduction strategies for viral hepatitis prevention.
Recognize the importance of nutrition-focused counseling in managing chronic liver disease in children.
Apply national guidelines like NVHCP for standardized prevention and management.
key_facts: Hepatitis B birth dose and primary series completion are critical for prevention.
The NVHCP provides India-specific guidance for hepatitis prevention and management.
Safe injection practices and avoiding shared razors/toothbrushes reduce household transmission risks.
Superinfection prevention includes safe water, food hygiene, and avoiding unnecessary herbal remedies.
Early care for symptoms like prolonged jaundice, pruritus, or poor weight gain is essential to prevent decompensation.
Evidence-based supportive care measures are essential
reference_quote: The MoHFW National Viral Hepatitis Control Programme (NVHCP) technical guidance provides the India-specific framework for hepatitis prevention, diagnosis, and management.
document_key_topics: Nutrition risk in chronic liver disease
